# Test validation functionality with mock responses
# Create a mock scenario for validation testing
-- validation_scenario.json --
{
  "name": "Validation Test",
  "description": "Test response validation",
  "steps": [
    {
      "name": "Simple Equality",
      "description": "Test simple equality matching",
      "request": {
        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
        "method": "test",
        "params": {},
        "id": 1
      },
      "expectations": [
        {
          "type": "response",
          "pattern": {
            "jsonrpc": "2.0",
            "result": {
              "success": true
            },
            "id": 1
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

# Create a mock responder script
-- mock_responder.sh --
#!/bin/bash
# Read input line by line
while read line; do
  # For any request, respond with success
  echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"success":true},"id":1}'
done

# Test validation with mock responder
! mcp-mock-client -scenario -validate validation_scenario.json | bash mock_responder.sh
stdout 'Running scenario: Validation Test
Description: Test response validation
Steps: 1
Step 1: Simple Equality'

# Test with verbose output to see validation success
chmod +x mock_responder.sh
mcp-mock-client -scenario -validate -v validation_scenario.json | ./mock_responder.sh
stdout 'Running scenario: Validation Test
Description: Test response validation
Steps: 1
Step 1: Simple Equality
  Description: Test simple equality matching
  Validating expectation 1: response
  Validation successful
Scenario completed successfully'

# Create a negative test scenario that should fail
-- failing_scenario.json --
{
  "name": "Failing Test",
  "description": "Test validation failure",
  "steps": [
    {
      "name": "Failing Match",
      "description": "This expectation should fail",
      "request": {
        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
        "method": "test",
        "params": {},
        "id": 1
      },
      "expectations": [
        {
          "type": "response",
          "pattern": {
            "jsonrpc": "2.0",
            "result": {
              "success": false
            },
            "id": 1
          },
          "fail_fast": true
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

# Test failing validation (should exit with non-zero status)
! mcp-mock-client -scenario -validate -v failing_scenario.json | ./mock_responder.sh
stdout 'Running scenario: Failing Test
Description: Test validation failure
Steps: 1
Step 1: Failing Match
  Description: This expectation should fail
  Validating expectation 1: response
  Validation failed'
! stdout 'Scenario completed successfully'
